title: impatiens leptopoda var. gibbosa
intro: Impatiens leptopoda var. gibbosa is a plant species commonly known as the golden-wattled balsam or golden-wattled balsam flower, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers with distinctive golden warts. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural remedy for skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Impatiens leptopoda var. gibbosa can be prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
